title: The Crew Faces the Kraken
description: On pages 16–19, this part follows Unknown person #1, Nami, Brook, Narrator, Usopp, Luffy, Zoro. The core story beat is the crew faces the kraken.
scene: The scene centers on the crew faces the kraken, with Unknown person #1, Nami, Brook, Narrator, Usopp, Luffy, Zoro driving the moment.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: D'you think there are any sea monsters around?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: ...Luffy... what exactly are you planning now...?!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Hey, Nami! Aren't we going in a slightly different direction to where the log pose is pointing?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: ...Dammitall... how could things turn-out so wrong?:?what am I gonna now?! I can hardly hope to fight them one- on-nine!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I'll be simple enough to break out of these ropes; but... I guess I could wait for my chance, and take 'em out one by one... or else...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: ...No, it's fine. Travelling slightly to the southwest of where the log pose points is the best route.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Why?! It'll be quicker if we just go straight where it's pointing, won't it?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Ahhh, I'm sprouting goosebumps all over my skin...!

dialogue.speaker: Brook
dialogue.text: You don't have any skin since you're just Bones!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Oh, but I...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: If we head straight for the island, we'll get caught in the currents...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...and be smashed against the mountains and volcanoes on the sea bed before we manage to reach the bottom.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Ehhhh?! Volcanoes?! The sea bed is scary!!!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: But I thought the currents in the Grand Line were totally unpredictable in the first place?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Is there really a route that'll guarantee we're safe?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: There is - just one...!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You should all put on coats like this, by the way. It's going to get cold from here on out.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We're going somewhere cold? I thought we were going to the sea bottom.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: idiot... of course it's gonna be cold at the bottom of the ocean! It's like a bath - the water at the bottom's always cooler than the water at the top!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Oh, right! So the bottom of the ocean would be colder than it is at the surface!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It is not only the temperature that is different! In the depths of the ocean...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: ...there are also what is called "Deeper currents"; entire currents of water that one would never be aware of from the surface

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: surface currents

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: These currents move in completely different directions to the "surface currents" that we are currently experiencing.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Deeper currents

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: You are here

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Huh?! There are currents underneath currents?? You sure know an awful lot!

dialogue.speaker: Brook
dialogue.text: Yohoho! I am your elder, after all!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: What's more,these currents are always interconnected somehow! And I don't just mean splitting off to the east and west or coming back together again...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: There are currents rising up to higher levels, or drilling deeper down... all connected together like some kind of giant dragon!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: These interconnected currents flow unbroken through the seas of the entire world!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: From what I have heard, the Deepwater currents flow along the sea bed at a rather leisurely pace... so slowly, in fact...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: ...that once a ship has travelled down to the deepest depths, it can take as much as two thousand years before they ever reach the surface again!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Two thousand years?!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Indeed... for this reason, many legends surround the dark, unexplored regions travelled by these deepwater currents...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Mighty beasts...!! Curses...!! The souls of the dead...!!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Whaaaaa?! There are ghosts at the bottom of the sea?!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: TH - There are?! That's so scary!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Now I'm getting excited!!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Well, who knows? The important thing is, we're getting on one of those currents that runs from the surface currents to the Deepwater levels!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: That way, we'll be taken straight down to the Deepwater currents and we can reach the sea bed!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Oh! You're right!! Man, you're really smart...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: But wait... how do we even find these... places where the water flows down??

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: It's just like what we were saying about the bathwater... where the water gets colder, the currents start to flow downward.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I get it!! So that's why we're heading into colder waters!!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The Red Line is essentially a huge collection of islands all joined together...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: There are "Summer Island" areas and "Winter Island" areas, just like anywhere else.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: So of course the area we're heading towards right now...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: ...is an ice-cold "winter Island" region.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: It's an area that fulfils the one other important condition for the currents to turn sharply downwards, as well.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Hm? And what on earth is this other condition, I wonder??

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: ...you always ask about these things without thinking, don't you...?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Are you sure you want to hear about Saline Density levels?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Salineden city? I think I might have been there as a kid...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: A slaying dynasty? Now that's something I'd like to see...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Ohhh, forget it!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: So zero, where did the bear guy send you?

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Oh, you're not gonna believe this...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: What's all this about Saline density?! Let us in on the secrets of the ocean!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Oh? You two are very enthusiastic! Well, basically, the conditions for water to flow downwards are that it has to be "cold" and "heavy"!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: In freezing-cold regions, you find ice forming in the ocean water, and when it does, the salt content...

summary: The Crew Faces the Kraken. This part advances the crew from their post-timeskip reunion into the undersea route and the first conflicts surrounding Fish-Man Island.
